question_text: Which of the following celestial bodies is correctly paired with its classification within the Solar System?

options.label: A
options.text: Jupiter is a terrestrial planet located in the inner Solar System

options.label: B
options.text: Mars is a gas giant with a thick atmosphere of hydrogen and helium

options.label: C
options.text: Earth is a terrestrial planet with a solid rocky surface

options.label: D
options.text: Neptune is a rocky planet in the inner Solar System

correct_answer: Earth is a terrestrial planet with a solid rocky surface
explanation: Earth is a terrestrial (or rocky) planet, one of the four inner planets of the Solar System along with Mercury, Venus, and Mars. These planets have solid rocky surfaces made of rock and metal, unlike the gas giants (Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune) in the outer Solar System.
